Comment: Adaptive Feedback-Controlled Micro-Batch Orchestrator for state-machine-amz-go. Splits a massive batch into sequentially-streamed micro-batches with Redis barrier-based fork-join and statistical failure-rate control. All Task states execute local Go functions registered on the executor. WaitForMicroBatchCompletion uses the framework's native Message state (pause/resume) instead of AWS Step Functions waitForTaskToken.

DispatchMicroBatch.Comment: Reads the Redis cursor, slices the next micro-batch of source-execution IDs from the keyIDsList, initialises the Redis barrier counter, and enqueues all tasks to the queue client. Returns isBatchComplete=true when all IDs have been dispatched.

CheckIfComplete.Comment: Routes to BatchCompleted when all micro-batches have been dispatched and there are no more IDs to process.

WaitForMicroBatchCompletion.Comment: Pauses the orchestrator using the framework's native Message-state pause/resume mechanism. CorrelationKey=micro_batch_id, CorrelationValue=$.dispatchResult.microBatchId. The last barrier worker calls parentSM.ResumeExecution which sends the correlation message and advances to EvaluateMicroBatch. TimeoutSeconds=86400 guards against hung workers.

Catch.Comment: All workers stalled or died; halt the orchestration.

EvaluateMicroBatch.Comment: Applies both statistical control measures: (1) instantaneous failure rate over last N tasks, (2) rolling average failure rate across last M micro-batches. Returns severeFailure=true or belowThreshold=true to trigger halt or pause respectively.

Choices.Comment: Instantaneous failure rate >= SevereFailureThreshold → halt immediately.
Rolling-average failure rate >= SoftFailureThreshold → pause for human review.

EvaluationDecision.Comment: Healthy → continue streaming to next micro-batch.

PauseBatch.Comment: Fixed back-off before polling for the operator resume signal. 30 seconds provides enough time for operators to be notified (via SNS/PagerDuty) and start investigating.

CheckResumeSignal.Comment: Polls the Redis resume key (GETDEL). If an operator called batch.Orchestrator.Signal(), the flag is atomically consumed and shouldResume=true is returned. Otherwise shouldResume=false and the loop returns to PauseBatch.

Catch.Comment: On transient error fall back into the poll loop rather than failing.

Choices.Comment: Operator cleared the issue → re-enter the dispatch loop.

BatchCompleted.Comment: All micro-batches have been dispatched and the final evaluation passed.

BatchFailed.Cause: The instantaneous failure rate exceeded SevereFailureThreshold, or a timeout/infrastructure error occurred. Inspect $.evaluation and $.error for details.
